pairs.question: Who won the 1960 United States presidential election in Ohio?
pairs.answer: Republican Party candidate Richard Nixon, the incumbent Vice President from California, won the 1960 presidential election in Ohio with 53.28% of the popular vote.

pairs.question: What percentage of the popular vote did John F. Kennedy receive in Ohio during the 1960 presidential election?
pairs.answer: John F. Kennedy, the Democratic candidate and Massachusetts Senator, received 46.72% of the popular vote in Ohio during the 1960 presidential election.

pairs.question: How many electors did Ohio send to the Electoral College in the 1960 presidential election?
pairs.answer: Ohio sent 25 electors to the Electoral College in the 1960 presidential election, who voted for President and Vice President.

pairs.question: Why was the 1960 Ohio election outcome notable for the state's historical voting patterns?
pairs.answer: The 1960 Ohio election was notable because it was the second time in the 20th century that Ohio voted for the losing presidential candidate. This anomaly was attributed to strong anti-Catholic voting in southern and western parts of the state, which were heavily influenced by Appalachian culture and Baptist communities.

pairs.question: What other election years did Ohio vote for the losing presidential candidate in the 20th century besides 1960 and 1944?
pairs.answer: Ohio did not vote for the losing candidate in any other 20th-century election besides 1944 and 1960. However, it backed the loser of the popular vote in 2000 (George W. Bush) and 2016 (Donald Trump).

pairs.question: Which counties in Ohio voted for the losing candidate in 1960 and did not do so again until 2020?
pairs.answer: Lawrence County voted for the losing candidate in 1960 and did not do so again until 2020. Ottawa County also voted for the losing candidate in 1960 and did not repeat this pattern until 2020.

pairs.question: How would Ohio's electoral history have changed if John F. Kennedy had won the state in 1960 and Franklin D. Roosevelt had won it in 1944?
pairs.answer: Had Kennedy won Ohio in 1960 and Roosevelt won it in 1944, Ohio would have held the longest streak of any state voting for the winning presidential candidate up until 2020.
